name: mods-toggle-and-remove
description: How to enable/disable, remove, or update an installed mod without hand-editing files — including isolating a crash-causing mod. Use when the user wants a mod toggled, deleted, or updated, or asks whether a specific mod is causing a crash ("禁用模组","删除模组","更新模组","是不是这个mod导致崩溃").
body:
# Toggle, remove, and update mods

`instance(action=mods_toggle, mod, enable, instance)` — enable/disable a mod file. Never
hand-rename `.jar`/`.jar.disabled` yourself; this keeps HMCL's mod list in sync.

`instance(action=mods_delete, mod, instance, DESTRUCTIVE)` — remove a mod (recycle-bins
when possible). Refuses on 0-or-multi-match names — run `mods_list` first for an exact id.

`instance(action=mods_check_updates, instance)` then `instance(action=mods_update, mod,
instance)` — update to the newest build instead of installing a duplicate copy
(duplicate jars are a classic crash cause).

## Crash isolation
1. Spot the suspect mod in the crash log/stack trace.
2. `instance(action=mods_toggle, mod, enable=false, instance)`, relaunch to confirm it stops.
3. Tell the user; offer to leave disabled or `mods_delete` — don't decide for them.

## Don't
- Don't rename mod files by hand — go through `mods_toggle`.
- Don't `mods_delete` an ambiguous name without checking `mods_list` first.
